programmes.show_description: A Moment of Madness: A blood-soaked man walks out of a hotel lift holding a fire extinguisher, shouting that he's killed someone. Detectives try to work out what's happened.
Seven-year-old Freddie is brought to St George's after falling from a tree in his garden. Martin, 66, has collapsed at home and is suffering from poor eyesight and dizziness.
Retired builder Roger has fallen four metres from his daughter's roof. And Tad, who's 29, is at St George's with a dangerously high heart rate, which could lead to a stroke.
The camping connoisseurs arrive at the magical Cefn Coed near the Mawddach Estuary, where forest bathing, a night in a traditional vardo caravan and a Thai cooking lesson await.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Gloucestershire, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet farmer Ken. In his barns, Henry finds a rare pre-WWII exercise bike and farmyard potato scales.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Brighton, Henry Cole salvages a kid's scooter from the junk in recycler Cat's sea containers. Simon O'Brien constructs the largest corkscrew that valuer Ronnie has ever seen.
Terence hosts at Becketts in Southsea, Portsmouth. His guests find the design delightful but the parking and a duvet disaster mean that one B&Ber's feedback isn't as flawless as expected.
At The Westby Guest House in Boscombe, Duncan and Jessie have a systematic approach to running a B&B. Although the rooms are impressive, Duncan's strict house rules soon leave a sour taste.
Paul and Tracey host at Newell Restaurant and Rooms in Dorset. Terence is taken with the blissful bedding, but Duncan's substandard slumber and the communal coffee zone prove controversial.
The B&Bers embrace the great outdoors at Emma and Rich's Lydcott Glamping in Cornwall. Everyone's taken with the spectacular surroundings but less so with their cabins and DIY breakfast.
The B&Bers meet up on payment day to settle their differences. There's plenty of finger pointing, before the payments are revealed and the winner announced.
Newcastle area. Ballroom dance instructor Stuart plans a rainbow-themed night with colourful food and a visit from one of the north east's most famous drag queens. (Ep1/5)
Newcastle area. Adam plans a menu of fine dining and gastropub grub. But some of his guests prove hard to please, including picky eater Harriet. (Ep2/5)
Newcastle area. Horse-mad nursing student Harriet throws a party themed around her equestrian hobby, including space hopper showjumping and a miniature pony. (Ep3/5)
Newcastle area. Videographer Jude hopes that her experimental vegetarian menu can win over her meat-eating guests, on a night that features a lost voice and a 'bottom-off'! (Ep4/5)
Newcastle area. After two nights with no voice, final host Mo is back on form with a menu of traditional South African dishes. (Ep5/5)
The competition begins at B&B at No.3 in Market Bosworth. Hosts Mac and Jo take pride in their Tudor architecture, but they've got a breakfast bombshell for their guests.
On day two, a topsy-turvy breakfast at Noreen and Chloe's establishment - The Bull Hotel in Buckinghamshire - leaves some of their guests happier than others...
At The Chequers Inn in Battle, East Sussex, Shaun and Kelly aim to please with their historic surroundings and homemade ingredients. But one guest delivers a shock in their feedback.
The last visit is to Hastings House in East Sussex, where Ian and Bev aim to provide an upmarket experience. But according to two guests, that doesn't extend to their towels...
The B&Bers sharpen their knives on payment day as they debate bad breakfasts, miserable mini bars and terrible towels, before the winning B&B is crowned.
In Bangor in Northern Ireland, Phil Spencer house-hunts with Lynn, who's been blind since birth. In Belfast, Kirstie Allsopp searches for a home to unite two busy doctors.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Newtownards, Amy and Brian are at loggerheads over their five-bed chalet bungalow. Can Kirstie make it feel like new again, or will the grass be greener elsewhere?
Brand new series - Deep Blue: A diver's death leads Blanca towards a crime ring trafficking Nazi memorabilia. And her love life gets more complicated. In Italian/subs.
Will, who's 13, gets a CT scan for head injuries after a baseball accident. Baby Amira has an irregular heartbeat. And pizza delivery rider Chris has been knocked off his bike.
Carole, who's 45, is brought to A&E after experiencing chest pains and collapsing. Philip, who's 53, is rushed in following a seizure. Doctors suspect he may have had a stroke.
In Oxfordshire, a motocross collision leaves 12-year-old Lewis with a broken thighbone. Paraglider Lucasz is treated for multiple fractures after a crash on the Cumbrian coast.
Will, who's 13, gets a CT scan for head injuries after a baseball accident. Baby Amira has an irregular heartbeat. And pizza delivery rider Chris has been knocked off his bike.
At Tyn-yr-Onnen Farm near Snowdonia National Park, the campers embrace farm life, from feeding alpacas and herding sheep to picking exotic mushrooms. But is it worth five stars?
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
The week begins at the Peniarth Arms in Wales, with hosts Susanne and Anwen. Toby and Kath feel like they've been transported in time, while Ryan and Sarah kick up a fuss about their room.
The second destination of the week is Apple Camping in Pembrokeshire. Host Toby's guests appreciate his unique take on glamping, but a lack of basics makes for some not-so-happy campers.
At The Garden House in Somerset, host and chef Ryan hopes that his brekkie will impress his guests. But some find the food difficult to stomach, resulting in some hard-to-swallow feedback.
James hosts the week's final visit at Ivy House in St Austell. His guests marvel at the delightful views, but the lack of a lock and a substandard sausage put James under scrutiny.
The B&Bers meet on payment day to settle some scores. Sleep troubles and breakfast bugbears are on the agenda, before the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Jasmine Harman helps Leigh from Kent look for a holiday home on stunning Tenerife. With a £300k budget, and the help of her nephew Sammy, can they find the one?
Best friends Tracey and Fran are pooling their finances to buy a holiday home together on the east coast of Fuerteventura. Can Danni Menzies find the perfect place for their £250k budget?
Horse lovers Mel and James want a holiday home in Mijas Pueblo in southern Spain. Laura Hamilton shows them what £180k can buy. Do any of the places leave Mel and James chomping at the bit?
Ben Hillman helps Joan and Andrew find their dream holiday home on Spain's Costa Cálida for the whole family to enjoy. With a budget of £80k, can they find the perfect place?
Gloucestershire. IT manager Bea serves up an international night inspired by her travels. But there's confusion over her Cretan feta and her geographical guessing game goes nowhere. (Ep1/5)
Gloucestershire. Wine connoisseur Adam plans to impress his guests with fine food and posh plonk. But will a custard catastrophe jeopardise his score? (Ep2/5)
Gloucestershire. On the third night, cycling enthusiast Steve plans an evening of globetrotting cuisine. But a hot sauce challenge leaves Adam red-faced. (Ep3/5)
Gloucestershire. Mum-of-four Lisa plans a night of 'pure entertainment', featuring back-to-basics grub and an interpretive dance challenge. (Ep4/5)
Gloucestershire. Hairdresser Lorna hosts the final night of the week, featuring a cremated main and some shocking revelations, before the winner is announced. (Ep5/5)
The week kicks off at Tess and Pete's seaside retreat, Rooms@119 in Ramsgate. Things don't get off to the best start, and their first-ever six-person breakfast doesn't go to plan either!
Mum-and-daughter duo Laurain and Kerry host the week's second visit, at Stable Oak Cottages in Maidstone, Kent. But will the DIY facilities and a messy bathroom cost them the competition?
Glamping is on the menu for the week's third visit, at Hedgerow Luxury Glamping in Clitheroe, where dairy farmer hosts Hillary and Mark plan to show their guests some high-end hospitality.
The final visit is to The Antelope Inn in Wiltshire, where hosts Natasha and Tanya strive for success. But unpleasant stains and a cold breakfast result in some rough feedback.
Payment day promises to be a tense affair, as the B&Bers take each other to task over some controversial comments. But which B&B has won the week?
The staff at St George's tackle one of their most dramatic and unusual emergencies. Rail worker Alan has been hit by a high-speed train and is at risk of losing his right arm.
Eleven-month-old Isabel is rushed to St George's after having a fit and becoming unconscious. And 22-year-old Felix is brought in with a self-inflicted stab wound.
An air ambulance team races to help Helen, who's trapped in her car after a crash on the A66. Teenager Jamie has a broken jaw and a possible spinal injury after falling 10 feet.
